characters.name: Baby Bottle
characters.description: Baby BottleAliasesN/ARelativesCuphead(Enemy)Mugman(Frienemy)Elder kettle(Enemy)AffiliationN/AOccupationA evil baby bottle with a red lid I guessBiographical informationMarital statusUnknownDate of birthUnknownPlace of birthA evil base maybeDate of deathUnknownPlace of deathUnknownPhysical descriptionSpeciesBaby bottle with glove and movesGenderNon BinaryHeightshortWeightshortEye colorblack pacman eyesAppearancesPortrayed byAnimatedAppears inBaby Bottle (episode)DebutCuphead (maybe)Baby Bottleis a minor character inThe Cuphead Show! Mugman(Frienemy)Elder kettle(Enemy) Baby Bottle's head is a baby bottle with what seems to be some sort of liquid inside (thought to be milk or beer), with a pinkish red cap. He has close-together pac-man eyes, and a small pink-nose in-between.

characters.name: Bear
characters.description: The Bear, or just Bear, is a minor character in The Cuphead Show! He was the one who drank the milk out of the empty baby bottle. When he revealed he talked, he huddled up with Cuphead, Mugman and Elder Kettle when the door knocked, finding the real Baby Bottle at the door. After holding him, Bear starting getting attacked and beaten up by the baby, asking from help from Cuphead, Mugman,  and Elder Kettle.

characters.name: Cuphead
characters.description: "Eh I ain't too worried about it." —Cuphead Cupheadis the main character in the cuphead show Cuphead is typically a nice person alongside his brotherMugman, enjoying an nice and good life, but is relentlessly annoying and reckless -and dumb at times-, his love for adventure can lead him to appear quite naughty, or get him into trouble, but at his core, he is kindhearted who wants to do good—but has the tendency to leap before he looks. Cuphead seems to be a daredevil. He is shown to be the more confident, fun-loving and slightly cheeky type. He often takes quite the amount of risks. His personality was mostly inspired by the "charming, yet trouble-making" character stereotype, as seen in the protagonists of early cartoons. As seen in the introduction, Cuphead seems to have a somewhat greedy personality, apparently willing to risk anything to get what he wants, as playing "Soul Ball" and being on "Roll The Dice" Despite his greediness, annoyingness, ect, ect, however, he seems to be kindhearted and has good intentions overall. He is shown to be very reckless, and often gets into fights with Mugman because "they're not supposed to". He also likes pancakes. It is unknown why his father-Elder kettle- had him andMugmanas children.

characters.name: Elder kettle
characters.description: Elder kettleAliasesUnknownRelativesCupheadMugmanMs. ChalicemaybeAffiliationN/AOccupationN/ABiographical informationMarital statusN/ADate of birthN/APlace of birthHis mom's houseDate of deathN/APlace of deathN/APhysical descriptionSpeciesOld man kettle thingyGenderMaleHeightN/AWeightN/AEye colorBlack paceman eyesAppearancesPortrayed byAnimatedAppears inSome episodesElder Kettleis a major secondary character ofThe Cuphead Show. He is the caretaker ofCupheadandMugman. Unlike his video game counterpart, Elder Kettle wears a small pair of glasses, a pair of red slippers and his cane as more of a derby design. Additionally, his colour scheme is sort of a greyey-blue shade, with his lid and stripe being more purpley. Elder Kettle has a comical side to him, and gets extremely upset whenever Cuphead and Mugman fight. Similarly, the show implies he's a retired war veteran and decorated war hero. He can turn hostile over slight misunderstandings, such as when he mistook the boys for zombies in the episodeGhosts Ain't Realor when he thinks the boys are plotting against him in the episode Dirt Nap. He is kind and generous, serving them pancakes for breakfast (and trying to give them a 'treat' which is painting the fence (boring)) in the episode Carnevil and healing Mugman's sadness for losing his handle when it got cracked off when playing blindfolded pillow fight in Handle With Care. The way he teaches Cuphead and Mugman stuff is quite crazy, because he can be very nice but also very angry, the most obvious example being in the episode Baby Bottle. He simply declares "Eh, boys will be boys" while the house is on fire, but gets reeeaaaallllly angry when he finds his radio destroyed. He always asks the boys to paint the fence, a thing they hate to do. Even because of this, he deeply cares about them and worries about what they think of him. characters.name: King Dice
characters.description: King DicePhysical descriptionGendermaleEye colorBlack Pacman EyesAppearancesPortrayed byAnimationAppears inRoll The DiceKing Diceis The Devil's right-hand man and self-proclaimed number one and a minor antagonist inThe Cuphead Show!He was also the former game host of the showRoll the Dice. He is voiced by Wayne Brady and Kazuya Nakai. InThe Cuphead Show!, his design stays the same as in the game, but with small changes. The buttons on his waistcoat are black, his bowtie is fuchsia purple, the collar of his tailcoat is in a darker shade of purple, and his eyelids are silver. His shoes also have a different design. King Dice's personality isn't really all that different from his original game counterpart. While much more charismatic than his portrayal in the game, he is no less deceitful and manipulative, as shown with his "prize" for the "winners" of his game show being them having their souls sucked out and sent to The Devil, and then having their bodies shoved out the door in front of a sign saying "thanks for playing." King Dice's personality isn't really all that different from his original game counterpart. While much more charismatic than his portrayal in the game, he is no less deceitful and manipulative, as shown with his "prize" for the "winners" of his game show being them having their souls sucked out and sent to The Devil, and then having their bodies shoved out the door in front of a sign saying "thanks for playing." Of course, unlike in the game, King Dice is more prone to underestimating Cuphead, as when he appeared on his show, he thought he would easily snatch up his soul for the Devil. This also brings out a more impatient and violent side to him, as Dice becomes incredibly angry and irritated from Cuphead's antics and tries to make him go into the "prize room" by brute force after reaching the end of his rope. King Dice is also shown to have an egotistical streak, even flirting with his own reflection before heading out on stage.

characters.name: Mugman
characters.description: Mugman is Cuphead's twin brother inThe Cuphead Show! MugmanStatusAlivePhysical descriptionEye colorBlack Pacman EyesAppearancesPortrayed byAnimationAppears inAll Episodes Mugman is very similar in overall appearance to his twin older brotherCuphead, with his head reminiscing a mug and his body being almost identical to his sibling: wearing a black, long-sleeve shirt, yellow gloves, a pair of shorts and brown shoes, although artworks sometimes show him with white gloves. He is also very girly, shown when babysitting Baby Bottle and being exciting at it calling him “mama”, Disgiusing himself as a bride when going out in Handle With Care, and knitting an invisible sweater for Cuphead in Sweater Off Dead.
characters.personality_section: He is also very girly, shown when babysitting Baby Bottle and being exciting at it calling him “mama”, Disgiusing himself as a bride when going out in Handle With Care, and knitting an invisible sweater for Cuphead in Sweater Off Dead.
